ogTitle: Surges Bay vs Surprise Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Surges Bay and Surprise Bay.
heading: Surges Bay vs Surprise Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Surges Bay, Tasmania and Surprise Bay, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Surges Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Surprise Bay (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Surges Bay or Surprise Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Surges Bay has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Surprise Bay scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Surges Bay scores 31/100 for bushfire risk. Surprise Bay scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Surges Bay or Surprise Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Surges Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
